caption_de:
Growing up in the western part of Germany, my computer journey started on my dad's IBM PC 5150 — editing config.sys and autoexec.bat just to make games run. I've always had a billion interests and a talent for getting distracted, so focusing on one thing was never my strong suit.

What actually pushed me into AI? Something pretty mundane: desktop wallpapers. Every single morning I was spending 30 to 60 minutes scrolling through free stock images, trying to find something that matched my mood, the weather, the season. One day I just thought — why not describe exactly what I want to an AI and let it generate the image for me?

The first results were... medium, honestly. But they were mine. They reflected what I was feeling in that moment, and somehow that meant more to me than any perfect stock photo. I started experimenting with Copilot Designer, DALL·E, and Midjourney. Each tool had its quirks — but I was hooked.

What happened next is where things started to get really interesting...

#digitalart #digipicshop #wallpaperdesign #aiart #desktopwallpaper #midjourney #generativeai #creativejourney

What actually pushed me into AI? Something pretty mundane: desktop wallpapers. Every single morning I was spending 30 to 60 minutes scrolling through free stock images, trying to find something that matched my mood, the weather, the season. One day I just thought — why not describe exactly what I want to an AI and let it generate the image for me?

The first results were... medium, honestly. But they were mine. They reflected what I was feeling in that moment, and somehow that meant more to me than any perfect stock photo. I started experimenting with Copilot Designer, DALL·E, and Midjourney. Each tool had its quirks — but I was hooked.
